message:
test(abi): tighten fchmodat2 + eventfd2 + rt_sigprocmask test invariants

Three small but meaningful refinements to the bd-dea1g test suite
shipped in 0c61ad9d, picking up the runtime_policy gate refactor
landed in the previous commit.

fchmodat2_null_path_returns_efault (new)
  - The runtime_policy refactor on fchmodat2 added an explicit
    NULL-pathname guard returning -1/EFAULT before the syscall is
    even attempted. Pin this contract — without it, a future
    refactor could silently start dispatching null pointers to the
    kernel and the existing fchmodat2_changes_mode_on_real_file
    test would not catch the regression.
  - assert_eq! on errno (not the |ENOSYS fallback) because the
    NULL guard is a pure ABI-side check that runs even on kernels
    where the underlying syscall is missing.

eventfd2_invalid_flags_return_einval (new)
  - eventfd2(0, !0) — every flag bit set, including ones the
    kernel reserves — must return -1 with EINVAL. Pins the
    "all-1s flags is rejected" contract that protects callers from
    accidentally enabling future kernel-only features by passing
    !0 instead of computing a real flag mask.
  - assert_eq! exact EINVAL — eventfd2 is in the system since
    Linux 2.6.27, so ENOSYS is not a tolerable outcome on any
    machine that can run this test suite.

rt_sigprocmask_round_trips_block_unblock (fix)
  - Drop the unused `empty: u64` local. The follow-up
    SIG_SETMASK call now restores from `prev` (the actual
    previous mask captured by the SIG_BLOCK call), which is what
    the test should have been doing in the first place — the old
    code restored to `empty = 0` and happened to work only
    because the test process started with no signals blocked.
    Restoring from `prev` makes the round-trip robust against any
    test ordering that ran rt_sigprocmask earlier in the same
    process.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
